title: abutilon mauritianum var. cabrae
intro: Abutilon mauritianum var. cabrae, commonly known as the Cape hollyhock, is a plant species in the mallow family (Malvaceae) characterized by its bright yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in South African and Indian folk medicine, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Abutilon mauritianum var. cabrae include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a diuretic and laxative. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or infusible extracts, and are often combined with other herbs to create remedies for various health conditions.
